channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What has Donald Trump not done yet? Here are some policy areas where he might act next
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Donald Trump’s second administration has put forth an avalanche of policy changes and political pronouncements that have jolted Washington and the world.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic AGs from 19 states sue Trump admin over DOGE access to sensitive, personal data at Treasury
item.description: A federal lawsuit filed by 19 Democratic state attorneys general claims Elon Musk's DOGE violated the law by accessing American's personal data from the Treasury Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transgender people sue Trump administration over new passport policy eliminating 'X' gender marker
item.description: A group of transgender individuals is suing the Trump administration over a new policy that eliminates the "X" gender marker on passports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Undocumented people can feel safe to report crimes, Nassau County executive says
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced Tuesday. Nassau County announced that 10 detectives will work with ICE.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: California approves $50M to protect immigrants and defend state against Trump administration
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom signed laws Friday setting aside $50 million to help the state protect its policies from challenges by the Trump administration and defend immigrants amid the president's mass-deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom signs $50M 'Trump-proofing' legislative package
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom called a special legislative session shortly after President Donald Trump's electoral victory to bolster the state's legal defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota joins lawsuit against Trump’s move to restrict gender-affirming care
item.description: Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison, along with attorneys general from Washington and Oregon and three unnamed doctors, filed a federal lawsuit Friday calling for a halt on a presidential executive order that would end federal funding to medical institutions that provide gender-affirming care.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: North Carolina judge's ruling retains close court election result, but more legal steps are expected
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — A North Carolina trial judge on Friday upheld decisions by election officials to reject protests by the trailing candidate in a very close state Supreme Court election who wants tens of thousands of contested ballots removed from the race tallies.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders freeze of aid to South Africa, citing country's land expropriation law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Friday formalizing his announcement earlier this week that he’ll freeze assistance to South Africa for a law aiming to address some of the wrongs of South Africa’s racist apartheid era — a law the White House says amounts to discrimi
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maxine Waters, House Dems ripped for 'unhinged' clash with security guard at Education Dept
item.description: House Democrats were ripped on social media after trying to gain entry into the Department of Education building in Washington, D.C., and accosting a security guard while doing so.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sotomayor criticizes presidential immunity case as putting the high court’s legitimacy on the line
item.description: In her first public comments since the start of Trump's second term, Justice Sotomayor said she believes the ruling puts the Court's legitimacy at risk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration withdraws support for transgender minors in Tennessee case at the Supreme Court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government no longer backs transgender minors and their families in Tennessee who are challenging a state ban on gender-affirming care, the Trump administration told the Supreme Court Friday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls for review of all US funding to NGOs
item.description: President Trump on Friday called for a review of federal funding to nongovernmental organizations (NGOs) and for a halt on spending to groups that “undermine the national interest.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats delay Kash Patel committee vote
item.description: Voting on Patel's nomination was on the agenda for Judiciary members, but Democrats requested it be held over one week.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: People gather at Minnesota Capitol in nationwide protest against President Trump
item.description: Protesters marched to the Capitol from St. Paul College, carrying signs denouncing policies on immigration, LGBTQ+ rights and federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina and Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Stauber reintroduces bill to reverse mining ban near Boundary Waters, return leases to Twin Metals
item.description: Northern Minnesota Republican Congressman Pete Stauber has reintroduced legislation to overturn a 20-year mining ban near the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. If passed, the bill would also return mineral leases to Twin Metals, the company vying to build an underground copper mine near Ely.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Bill count swells to 484 as Nevada Legislature hits Day 2; Voter ID a Republican goal
item.description: Packed binders. Busy halls. Lobbyists, lawmakers and journalists roaming amongst each other. This is when the routine of the state government sets in. Already, there are 484 bills submitted this session, with many more expected.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. appears on track to become US health secretary as he wins key Republican senator's support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal vaccine skeptic and activist lawyer, appeared on track to become the nation's health secretary after winning the crucial support of Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nassau police to help ICE detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes
item.description: NASSAU COUNTY, N.Y. (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC
